questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In 2023, the UN General Assembly requested an advisory opinion from the International Court of Justice (ICJ) specifically concerning which of the following areas?

options.text: The legality of nuclear weapons in armed conflicts
States' obligations under international law to protect the environment
The definition of maritime boundaries in the South China Sea
The legal status of non-state actors in international trade disputes

questions.explanation: In 2023, the UN General Assembly adopted a resolution requesting the ICJ to issue an advisory opinion on states' obligations under international law to protect the environment and the legal consequences for failing to meet these obligations.
questions.takeaway: The 2023 ICJ advisory opinion request focuses on environmental protection and the legal consequences of climate-related failures.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: In 2023, the UN General Assembly adopted a resolution requesting the ICJ to issue an advisory opinion on: States' obligations under international law to protect the environment; Legal consequences for failing to meet these obligations.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Regarding the composition and election of judges to the International Court of Justice (ICJ), which of the following is correct?

options.text: The court consists of 15 judges elected for a non-renewable term of 5 years.
Judges are elected solely by the UN General Assembly to ensure democratic representation.
The court is composed of 15 judges elected for 9-year terms by the UN General Assembly and Security Council.
Judges are appointed by the UN Secretary-General from a pool of international legal experts.

questions.explanation: The ICJ is composed of 15 judges who are elected for 9-year terms. The election process involves both the UN General Assembly and the Security Council.
questions.takeaway: ICJ judges serve 9-year terms and are elected through a dual-body process involving the UNGA and UNSC.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Composition: 15 judges elected for 9-year terms by the UN General Assembly and Security Council.
